steps.title: Find what the passage argues about disco
steps.content:
The passage says disco is "far less superficial than many people believe" and uses the parenthetical "subjects hardly unique to disco, by the way" to signal that dismissing disco for love songs is unfair — those same subjects appear across all genres. Donna Summer also addressed community and identity, showing the genre has real substance.

With that in mind, ask: which choice matches the idea that disco has been wrongly dismissed based on a flaw that isn't even specific to disco?
  • Choice A — 'It has been unjustly ignored by most scholars despite the importance of the themes addressed by many of the genre's songs.': scholars actively engaged with disco, arguing it is not superficial. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It evolved over time from a superficial genre focused on romance to a genre focused on more serious concerns.': the text says the serious themes were always there, not that the genre changed. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It has been unfairly dismissed for the inclusion of subject matter that is also found in other musical genres.': the parenthetical directly says love songs are "hardly unique to disco" — dismissing disco for them is unfair. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It gave rise to a Black women's musical tradition that has endured even though the genre itself faded in the late twentieth century.': the text says Black women singers preceded disco, not that disco launched a new tradition. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
The correct answer is the only one supported by the text: disco was ridiculed for themes that appear in every genre, making the dismissal unfair.

That is the correct answer.
